query: What is the current national norovirus category on the WastewaterSCAN dashboard as of today (High/Medium/Low), and is it rising, falling, or stable? Note the unusual summer 2026 GII.17 off-season surge.
answer:
## Summary

**Current national norovirus status on WastewaterSCAN (as of July 7, 2026): MEDIUM-to-HIGH and RISING.**

**Key findings:**

- **Last official WastewaterSCAN monthly update** (data through May 30, 2026) showed the national category at **MEDIUM, falling** — the receding tail of the 2025–26 winter wave (99% sample detection; concentration down ~31% from April; sites split 34 Low / 55 Medium / 50 High).

- **June–July 2026 reporting** citing the dashboard describes an **unusual off-season summer surge**: signals **rising toward "HIGH"** across much of the U.S., **rising fastest in the Northeast**, with activity in ≥12 states (Utah, Texas, Ohio, North Carolina, Pennsylvania leading) and a **peak expected around July 2026**.

- **GII.17 context:** This immune-evasive genotype ("Ferrari of viruses") overtook GII.4 as the dominant U.S. strain in 2024–25 (~75% of outbreaks, up from <10% in 2022–23). Its earlier/prolonged seasonality is the leading explanation for off-season activity, making a summer surge plausible.

**Two important caveats:**
1. The live WastewaterSCAN badge is JavaScript-rendered and **could not be scraped directly**, so today's exact category should be confirmed at data.wastewaterscan.org. The MEDIUM→HIGH/rising read is reconstructed from the official newsletters plus contemporaneous news.
2. The **direct GII.17 → summer 2026 surge link is inferential**, not yet confirmed in a WastewaterSCAN bulletin — the July-specific consumer sources describe the surge but do not explicitly name the genotype; GII.17 attribution comes from CDC CaliciNet/NoroSTAT covering the prior season.

Structured findings have been recorded.

sources.snippet: 1,287 outbreaks reported Aug 1, 2025–Jun 11, 2026, below prior season.

sources.title: CaliciNet Data | Norovirus | CDC
sources.snippet: Genotype distribution Sep 1, 2025–May 31, 2026 (n=131) showing GII.17 predominance.

sources.title: Norovirus Surge May Be Driven by Ultra-Contagious Variant
sources.snippet: Norovirus surging; ties to ultra-contagious GII.17 variant; cites WastewaterSCAN.
